ps140: 4, Podoben “As one valiant among the martyrs…”

Perceiving you, O blessed martyr Julian,* as a brilliant sun* illumining the fullness of the world* with noetic splendours,* we celebrate your radiant* and divine memory,* and bow down before the shrine of your relics,* drawing forth health** for our souls.

Beaten, wounded and grievously scourged,* imprisoned in a dungeon,* O blessed one,* driven from place to place,* caged with wild beasts,* brought to the abyss and drowned in the sea,* you received a blessed end,* refusing to deny the Master of all,** O glory of the martyrs, who are most rich.

Cast up upon dry land* out of the bosom of the sea* by the action of the Spirit,* you were seen by an honourable woman,* who faithfully took you up, O martyr Julian,* and committed to burial* your immaculate and much-suffering body,* which had vanquished the tyranny of the devil** through the workings of grace.

Having drawn forth the inexhaustible drink of the immaculate Faith, O blessed one,* you extinguished the worship of idols,* and victoriously passed through the contest, made luminous with dew,* shining like a never-waning star of Christ, the ever-shining Sun;* and, emitting rays of martyrdom in each city,* you received a blessed end in the sea,* and came before the face of Christ as a crown-bearer.* Beseech Him, O all-glorious Julian,** that He save those who celebrate your memory with faith.
stavrotheotokion: Of old, when the unblemished ewe-lamb, the immaculate Mistress,* beheld her Lamb upon the Tree of the Cross,* she exclaimed maternally and cried out in wonder:* “O my Child most sweet, what new and strange thing is this that I see?* How have the thankless multitude given You over to the tribunal of Pilate* and condemned You, the Life of all, to death?** Yet I hymn Your ineffable condescension, O Word!”
aposticha: Come, O lovers of the martyrs,* let us piously glorify the glorious Julian,* the passion-bearer for Christ,* the compatriot of Paul,* and proved himself to be his fellow-initiate,* and who finished the race with him.* For, entering the water, he crushed the head of the serpent with the aquatic creatures,* sanctifying the earth with his blood and the sea by his passage,* passing from things here to the eternal dwellings,* and receiving honour from the hand of the Almighty for his feats.** And he asks for cleansing for our souls and for great mercy.
stavrotheotokion_aposticha: Standing with the virginal disciple before the Tree during the crucifixion,* the Virgin cried out, weeping:* “Woe is me! How is it that You suffer, O Christ,** since You are the dispassion of all?”
